summary: Delete function mongoc_collection_save
description: Move closer to CRUD Spec compliance and uniformity with other drivers.

summary: Specify behavior of empty authSource URI option
Error if URI has username and no password for MONGODB-AWS
description:
*Summary*

Error if URI has username and no password for MONGODB-AWS

*Background & Motivation*

Tests for the MONGODB-AWS auth mechanism were added in SPEC-1515 but missed when implementing MONGODB-AWS.

The following MONGODB-AWS test fails:

{code:json}
{
  "description": "should throw an exception if username and no password (MONGODB-AWS)",
  "uri": "mongodb://user@localhost/?authMechanism=MONGODB-AWS",
  "valid": false,
  "credential": null
}
{code}

The expected error is not returned. As a workaround, this test is planned to be skipped (search code for this ticket number).

summary: Return default $external auth source for MONGODB-AWS
description:
*Summary*

Change the return of {{mongoc_uri_get_auth_source}} to return "$external" for MONGODB-AWS to agree with the applied auth source.

*Background & Motivation*

Tests for the MONGODB-AWS auth mechanism were added in SPEC-1515 but missed when implementing MONGODB-AWS.

Many MONGODB-AWS connection string tests fail. Example:
{code:json}
{
  "description": "should recognise the mechanism (MONGODB-AWS)",
  "uri": "mongodb://localhost/?authMechanism=MONGODB-AWS",
  "valid": true,
  "credential": {
    "username": null,
    "password": null,
    "source": "$external",
    "mechanism": "MONGODB-AWS",
    "mechanism_properties": null
  }
}
{code}
The expected "source" is "$external" to agree with the [specified default|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/master/source/auth/auth.md#mongocredential-properties-5]:
{quote}source

MUST be "$external". Defaults to $external.
{quote}
However, {{mongoc_uri_get_auth_source}} returns "admin". Internally, "$external" [is hardcoded|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/70d204fd3b46d710894e2f94127567c0a69dae86/src/libmongoc/src/mongoc/mongoc-cluster-aws.c#L67] for MONGODB-AWS auth. As a workaround, this test is planned to be skipped (search code for this ticket number).

summary: mongoc_server_description_host should return const pointer
Remove mongoc_cursor_is_alive
Remove minPoolSize option
description: See CDRIVER-2389: minPoolSize is flawed, remove it in 2.0.

summary: Remove mongoc_client_kill_cursor
Remove automatic init/cleanup
Prevent re-execution of bulk ops
description:
Contradicting the Bulk API spec, the driver allows multiple calls to the same mongoc_bulk_operation_execute ().

If the bulk operation is only updates or removes then they'll be run twice. If inserts are included, the driver prepends object ids to the documents for insertion before the first execution, so the inserts fail with duplicate key error on the second and subsequent executions.

API change, can't do it until 2.0.

summary: Remove BSON_EXTRA_ALIGNMENT option
description: A few structs like bson_t and bson_iter_t are declared with 128-byte alignment instead of accepting the compiler default. It's intended to enforce efficient use of the CPU cache, but it leads to obscure problems: CDRIVER-2810, CDRIVER-596, CXX-CXX-1678. Remove the flag and the alignment specifiers.

summary: Disallow comma character in authMechanismProperties connection string value
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2915}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Mark bson-memory functions with __cdecl
description: This [pull request|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/509] was opened to change the bson_mem_vtable_t and realloc function pointers to mark as __cdecl. This would be an ABI break, so we can't fix it until 2.0.0. If/when we do fix this, re-open that PR and merge.

summary: mongoc_log_func_t is missing __cdecl
Prohibit setting options on pooled clients
description: If you check out a client from a pool, set any options on it (read pref, write concern, SSL), then check it back in, the pool now contains a mix of clients with different configurations.

summary: pkg-config file libmongoc-ssl-1.0.pc isn't necessary
description: All that libmongoc-ssl-1.0.pc does is provide a default pkg-config file that depends on libmongoc-1.0.pc - it's effectively an alias for libmongoc-1.0.pc. Mark it deprecated and remove it in 2.0.

summary: Ensure stability of resumeTokens across versions
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-3058}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Fix format warning on Windows
description: Tracks: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/1972

summary: Partial result may be populated after client-side error in first client bulk write operation
description:
h4. Summary

In [CRUD prose test 12|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.30.2/src/libmongoc/tests/test-mongoc-crud.c#L1174], which asserts client-side errors from the first and only write modle, a partial result is still populated in {{mongoc_bulkwritereturn_t.res}}. This seemingly runs afoul of the following line in the spec:

bq. Drivers MUST NOT populate the partialResult field in BulkWriteException if it cannot be determined that at least one write was successfully performed.

Looking at [{{mongoc_bulkwrite_execute}}|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/1.30.2/src/libmongoc/src/mongoc/mongoc-bulkwrite.c#L1949], this may be due to assuming {{has_successful_results = true}} if {{ret.res->errorscount == 0}} for an ordered write. {{errorscount}} is only incremented in {{_bulkwritereturn_apply_reply}}, which presumably would never have been called.

summary: Username derived from x509 certs on Windows may include extra comma
description:
*Summary*

The C driver may incorrectly [include an extra comma|https://github.com/kevinAlbs/mongo-c-driver/blob/56a89c5359c165420cdca5df4652737cb8c80886/src/libmongoc/src/mongoc/mongoc-secure-channel.c#L274] in the derived X509 username on Windows. This can cause an authentication failure and require the username be specified in the URI as a workaround.

*How to Reproduce*

To reproduce:
 - Create a cluster in Atlas with X509 auth.
 - Download the certificate for the user.
 - Re-encrypt the certificate from PKCS#8 to PKCS#1 to workaround CDRIVER-4269
 - Try to connect with the C driver with no username specified.

Example on Cygwin for a user "test-user":
{noformat}
# Include path to libmongoc and libbson dlls:
export PATH="$PATH:$(cygpath -u "C:\cygwin\home\Administrator\code\mongo-c-driver\cmake-build\src\libbson\Debug")"
export PATH="$PATH:$(cygpath -u "C:\cygwin\home\Administrator\code\mongo-c-driver\cmake-build\src\libmongoc\Debug")"


# Path to client certificate in PKCS#1:
CLIENTCERT_PATH="C:\cygwin\home\Administrator\code\mongo-c-driver\KEVINALBS\X509-cert-8204221885429283915.pem"


URI="mongodb+srv://$DOMAIN/?authSource=%24external&authMechanism=MONGODB-X509&tlsCertificateKeyFile=$CLIENTCERT_PATH"
./cmake-build/src/libmongoc/Debug/mongoc-ping.exe $URI
# Fails with "Authentication failed"
# Atlas server logs show extra comma incorrectly sent with username: ",CN=test-user"


# Specify username to workaround derivation bug:
URI="mongodb+srv://CN=test-user@$DOMAIN/?authSource=%24external&authMechanism=MONGODB-X509&tlsCertificateKeyFile=$CLIENTCERT_PATH"
./cmake-build/src/libmongoc/Debug/mongoc-ping.exe $URI
# Succeeds!
{noformat}
The derived username is expected to be "CN=test-user", the C driver derives it as ",CN=test-user".

*Workarounds*

Specify the username for X509, rather than using the driver derived username. Example: instead of:
{noformat}
mongodb+srv://host/?authSource=%24external&authMechanism=MONGODB-X509&tlsCertificateKeyFile=cert.pem
{noformat}
Use:
{noformat}
mongodb+srv://CN=user@host/?authSource=%24external&authMechanism=MONGODB-X509&tlsCertificateKeyFile=cert.pem
{noformat}

summary: Username derived from x509 certs on macOS has different order of RDNs
Gossip cluster time from internal MongoClient to session entities
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2816}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Authentication Failure messages logged at DEBUG level, should be ERROR or CRITICAL
description:
*Summary*

When encountering authentication failures using the C driver, we see the failures marked with a DEBUG log level, e.g.:
{quote}Mongo C driver event type: DEBUG
Event text: "cluster: Authentication failed: Authentication failed."
{quote}
or
{quote}Mongo C driver event type: DEBUG
Event text: "cluster: Authentication failed: SASL Failure: ..."
{quote}
Our code does not force an error in the case of DEBUG messages. We only do that for ERROR and CRITICAL log levels which we think would be more appropriate for this type of connection failure. 

*Versions*

We see this in all tested versions of the C driver including 1.14.0 and 1.20.0. We are testing on x86 Linux (RHEL 7, RHEL 8). Our tests are running against 3.6 and 6.0 MongoDB database versions. 

*Reproducing:*

Example connection strings pulled from a trace of our code:
{quote}connect string: mongodb://username%40INTRANET.customer.COM:XXXXXXXX@hostname.intranet.customer.com:27180/odsCustomers?ssl=true&authMechanism=GSSAPI&authSource=$external&gssapiServiceName=sysmngtcustlett&socketTimeoutMS=-1
        code=1000
        code=1000, errnum=B172, errid=password_decryption_error
Mongo message: DEBUG: cluster: Authentication failed: Authentication failed.
{quote}

summary: Fix message of bson_strerror_r on Windows
description: Tracks: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/2011

summary: Investigate whether allocated PCCERT_CONTEXT is freed
description:
While looking into CDRIVER-3227 I realized that the {{PCCERT_CONTEXT}} pointer, which is initialized by [CertSetCertificateContextProperty|https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/wincrypt/nf-wincrypt-certsetcertificatecontextproperty] in {{mongoc_secure_channel_setup_certificate_from_file}}, is never freed.

Based on [this program example|https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/seccrypto/example-c-program-getting-and-setting-certificate-properties] in Microsoft's documentation, it looks like we should free the pointer with [CertFreeCertificateContext|https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/wincrypt/nf-wincrypt-certfreecertificatecontext].

I do see that the allocated {{PCCERT_CONTEXT}} is later passed to {{AcquireCredentialsHandle}} via the {{SCHANNEL_CRED}} struct, so perhaps it is ultimately freed later on. If so, there may be nothing to fix here.

summary: Upgrade Python tornado package to 6.5.0
description:
Address "High" severity Dependabot alerts concerning the {{tornado}} package (required by the {{sphinx-autobuild}} package) by upgrading to version 6.5.0 which addresses [Alert 21|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/security/dependabot/21] (fixed in 6.4.2) and [Alert 26|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/security/dependabot/26] (fixed in 6.5.0).

The {{sphinx-autobuild}} package is a "for development only" package which should not affect downstream users' typical use of the C Driver repository, but this is nevertheless a worthwhile opportunity to update the Poetry lockfile, given it was last updated on Oct 2024.

summary: Improve error messages from Windows APIs
description: Tracks: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/2020

summary: Reduce Warnings - miscellaneous MSVC warnings
description: Tracks https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/1992.

summary: Unable to load PKCS#8 private key with SChannel
description:
h4. Summary

Private keys can be in PKCS#1 or PKCS#8 format. Secure Channel on Windows is unable to load PKCS#8 encoded private keys.
h4. How to Reproduce

Start a server and require client certificates. Use test .pem files from the [https://github.com/mongodb-labs/drivers-evergreen-tools] repo:
{noformat}
./bin/mongod \
    --setParameter enableTestCommands=1 \
    --tlsCertificateKeyFile=C:/cygwin/home/Administrator/code/drivers-evergreen-tools/.evergreen/x509gen/server.pem \
    --tlsCAFile=C:/cygwin/home/Administrator/code/drivers-evergreen-tools/.evergreen/x509gen/ca.pem \
    --tlsMode=requireTLS \
    --port=27018 \
    --dbpath ./data
{noformat}
Use the [repro environment here|https://github.com/kevinAlbs/cxx-bootstrap/blob/ec6260c13a4c4bf484975e880383265d00dbcf35/investigations/help30888/crepro.c]:

Use the [run.sh|https://github.com/kevinAlbs/cxx-bootstrap/blob/master/investigations/help30888/run.sh] script to test connecting with a PKCS#1 key (succeeds) and a PKCS#8 private key (fails):
{noformat}
export CA_FILE="C:/cygwin/home/Administrator/code/drivers-evergreen-tools/.evergreen/x509gen/ca.pem"
export CLIENT_FILE="C:/cygwin/home/Administrator/code/drivers-evergreen-tools/.evergreen/x509gen/client-pkcs8-unencrypted.pem"
export MONGODB_URI="mongodb://localhost:27018/?tls=true&tlsCAFile=$CA_FILE&tlsCertificateKeyFile=$CLIENT_FILE"
./cmake-build/Debug/crepro.out
{noformat}
Gets the following output on Windows:
{noformat}
2022/01/25 23:59:03.0700: [ 9916]:    ERROR: stream-secure-channel: Failed to parse private key. ASN1 bad tag value met.
(0x8009310B)
2022/01/25 23:59:03.0930: [ 9916]:    ERROR: stream-secure-channel: Failed to parse private key. ASN1 bad tag value met.
(0x8009310B)
2022/01/25 23:59:03.0954: [ 9916]:    ERROR:       mongoc: mongoc_collection_count_documents error: No suitable servers found (`serverSelectionTryOnce` set): [Failed to receive length header from server. calling hello on 'localhost:27018']
{noformat}
h4. Additional Background

See SERVER-35541 for an example of this implementation in the MongoDB Server.

See [https://stackoverflow.com/a/48960291/774658] for additional background on PKCS#1 and PKCS#8 format.

[https://github.com/jeroen/mongolite/issues/266] may be a related case.

*Workaround*

As a workaround, users can convert PKCS#8 to PKCS#1 for an RSA key. In OpenSSL 3.0+:
{noformat}
openssl pkey -in private_key_pkcs8.pem -traditional
{noformat}
 
In OpenSSL versions less than 3:
{noformat}
openssl rsa -in private_key_pkcs8.pem -out private_key_pkcs1.pem
{noformat}

summary: Support auto encryption in unified tests
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-3106}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: bson_validate functions are broken by strings/keys that are not valid UTF-8
description:
The {{BSON_VALIDATE_UTF8}} flag requests that the {{bson_validate}} family of functions check the content of UTF-8 text elements for byte sequences that are not valid UTF-8. This flag never has any effect, because the {{bson_iter_visit_all}} function checks for invalid UTF-8 before the validating visitor gets a chance to inspect the element content.

{{bson_iter_visit_all}} sets an {{err_off}} on the iterator, but the validation functions do not check this value. Instead, they *halt early* on invalid UTF-8 sequences, and then report success. This is also a problem when keys contain invalid UTF-8, because the visitor still does not see the element and does not inspect {{{}iter.err_off{}}}.

summary: Docs build failure with Sphinx 8.2+ when using the dirhtml builder
description:
h4. Summary

This is a minor build-time issue found by downstream packagers. When building docs with {{{}dirhtml{}}}, API changes in Sphinx 8.2 (October, 2024) cause the docs build to fail.
h4. Environment

This does not occur if using a Poetry-installed version of Sphinx, because we have it pinned to 7.4. Installing Sphinx 8.2 or newer is required to reproduce. The issue only occurs in the {{dirhtml}} builder. The issue does not reproduce in EVG because it defaults to using the plain {{html}} builder.
h4. How to Reproduce

Install Sphinx 8.2 or newer, and run the docs build with {{{}-b dirhtml{}}}. The build will fail in {{mongoc_common.py:generate_html_redirs}}
h4. Additional Background

Downstream bug report: [https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/mongo-c-driver/+bug/2113467]

Breaking Sphinx commit: [https://github.com/sphinx-doc/sphinx/commit/285908aa073e5320d5f5a8b4648a2265c76a3f32]

External PR: [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/2033]

summary: Prevent multi-evaluation of arithmetic macro-function operands
description: Tracks: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/1999

summary: Record both FaaS and container metadata when both are present
description: This ticket was split from {{DRIVERS-2830}}, please see that ticket for a detailed description.

summary: Use thread-safe pseudo-random number generator API
description: Tracks: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/2065

summary: Do not persist large stream buffers
SChannel client auth fails if server does not support SHA1
description:
h1. Summary

The C driver built with Secure Channel (default on Windows) fails to auth with MONGODB-X509 to a server not accepting RSA+SHA1 signature.
h2. Workarounds

Build C driver with OpenSSL (using the CMake option {{{}-DENABLE_SSL=OPENSSL{}}}). This requires obtaining OpenSSL separately.

summary: Restore cmake_policy in GenerateUninstaller.cmake
description:
[mongodb/mongo-c-driver#2066|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/2066] removed a call to {{cmake_policy}} in GenerateUninstaller.cmake, which is required when the script is invoked via the uninstall target in CMake script mode.

Tracks: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/2070

summary: Uninstall batch scripts return a non-zero exit code due to self-deletion
description: Caused by [070b35a|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/commit/070b35ab163230871c37dd4c8262c05c423e1d54] (August 2023 in the 1.25.0 release). Although the uninstallation operation itself works as-designed, the operation may return a non-zero exit code due to the batch script being deleted mid-execution [despite attempts|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/070b35ab163230871c37dd4c8262c05c423e1d54/build/cmake/GenerateUninstaller.cmake#L135-L139] to workaround this issue.

summary: bson_get_monotonic_time has 10-16 millisecond resolution on Windows
description:
h4. Summary

{{bson_get_monotonic_time()}} uses [{{GetTickCount64}}|https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/sysinfoapi/nf-sysinfoapi-gettickcount64] on Windows, which appears to have a resolution in the range of 10-16 milliseconds. This is actually quite a bit worse than what I originally assumed reading the libbson code, which implies that the returned value is just milliseconds.

This came up while fixing some command monitoring tests on Windows for PHPC-2143. We had a few tests that assert a non-zero duration for a commandFailed or commandSucceeded event, and I noticed that these tests occasionally fail (presumably when the calculated duration is less than {{GetTickCount64}}'s resolution.

Given this limitation, is there an alternative that would make more sense on Windows? Is command monitoring the only likely case where diffing two {{bson_get_monotonic_time()}} return values might evaluate to zero? If not, perhaps {{bson_gettimeofday}} would make a better fallback. 

h4. Environment

 * libmongoc 1.23.1
 * Windows Server 2019 64-bit
 * MSVC15 (Visual C++ 2017) x64

h4. How to Reproduce

Example script: [commandSucceededEvent-001.phpt|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/blob/1.14.2/tests/apm/commandSucceededEvent-001.phpt]

summary: Begin Header Hygiene & Verification
description: Tracks https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/pull/2053. Make changes to work towards header hygiene goals described in CDRIVER-6085.

summary: mongoc_collection_create_indexes_with_opts does not apply write command behavior
Single-threaded monitoring commands may include saslSupportedMechs beyond initial handshake
mongoc_client_reset should do nothing if passed a multi-threaded client
description:
Currently {{mongoc_client_reset}} clears the session pool on the client's topology by calling {{_mongoc_topology_clear_session_pool}}. This is fine for a single-threaded client, but not ok for a client derived from a {{mongoc_client_pool_t}} since the topology object is shared among all pooled clients, and no lock is taken.

We decided not to implement a {{mongoc_client_pool_reset}} in CDRIVER-2857 (relevant comment).

{{mongoc_client_reset}} should probably warn and return without doing anything if given a multi-threaded client.

Let's also document in {{mongoc_client_reset}} that this only applies to single threaded clients.

summary: Do not resume after change stream cursor is closed
description:
h4. Summary

Attempting to iterate a closed change stream results in the server error:
{noformat}Attempting to resume a change stream using 'resumeAfter' is not allowed from an invalidate notification.
{noformat}
Proposed Change: Do not attempt to resume the change stream. Return an error message indicating the change stream is closed.

Consider: Compare behavior with other driver behavior when iterating a closed change stream cursor. Consider filing a DRIVERS ticket to specify a change stream must not attempt to resume after closed.
h4. How to Reproduce

This branch includes a failing test to reproduce the error: [https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/compare/master...kevinAlbs:mongo-c-driver:changestream]
h4. Additional Background

The server signals a change stream is closed by responding with a 0 cursor ID. Example:
{noformat}{ "cursor" : { "id" : 0, "ns" : "db.coll", "nextBatch" : [  ], "postBatchResumeToken" : { "_data" : "826516E9230000000F2B042C0100296F5A1004A9D70E866B784600A7165560BE721BF2463C6F7065726174696F6E54797065003C64726F70000004" } }, "ok" : 1.0, "$clusterTime" : { "clusterTime" : { "$timestamp" : { "t" : 1696000291, "i" : 23 } }, "signature" : { "hash" : { "$binary" : { "base64" : "AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA=", "subType" : "00" } }, "keyId" : 0 } }, "operationTime" : { "$timestamp" : { "t" : 1696000291, "i" : 23 } } }
{noformat}

The C driver {{mongoc_cursor_t}} returns this error when iterating a closed {{mongoc_cursor_t}}:
{noformat}
Cannot advance a completed or failed cursor
{noformat}

The Go driver {{ChangeStream}} [checks for a cursor ID of 0|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver/blob/cd3c6996acf2db8eb02df0966fa6f76168ab1abf/mongo/change_stream.go#L664-L667]. returns {{false}} to signal no events are returned, and does not attempt to resume or return an error.

summary: Collection bulk write with let loses let if document gets too big
Support TLS v1.3 in Secure Channel
description:
Enable TLS 1.3 in the C driver when using Windows Secure Channel as the TLS implementation (default on Windows).
h1. Background & Motivation

The Windows Secure Channel TLS implementation [does not enable TLS 1.3|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-c-driver/blob/fa7028202000674940651d4bdbdc04929ba4af2e/src/libmongoc/src/mongoc/mongoc-stream-tls-secure-channel.c#L932].

Windows server 2022 [supports TLS 1.3|https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/secauthn/protocols-in-tls-ssl--schannel-ssp-].

libcurl may help as [reference|https://github.com/curl/curl/blob/31a7905ce18496bfbe0b1e5d1e6d50a4b09ce63b/lib/vtls/schannel.c#L789]. From experimenting, it appears two changes are needed:
 * Handle renegotiation (currently [does nothing|https://github.com/kevinAlbs/mongo-c-driver/blob/1b0e008d3aab07431827433c783f92680cb0cffe/src/libmongoc/src/mongoc/mongoc-stream-tls-secure-channel.c#L537]?).
 * Switch from SCHANNEL_CRED to SCH_CREDENTIALS.

The MongoDB server does not yet support TLS v1.3 on Windows (SERVER-98279). But I expect supporting TLS v1.3 will improve the security posture, and may be a future need if users that disable older TLS protocols.

summary: MLIB_IS_EMPTY fails on function macro accepting args
GridFS bucket allocation unchecked
Check Cyrus username length
